plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-393 RP13.P01.M06.S01-RP13.P02.M04.S06 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-393 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP13.P01 model foundation phase (M06 Synthetic Fixture Set, M07 Test And Golden Case Set, M08 Hermes Evidence Packet, M09 Claude Review Packet, M10 Closeout And Next Handoff) and opens the RP13.P02 foundation (M00 Scope Inventory, M01 Contract Draft, M02 Type And Shape Definition, M03 Primary Implementation Slice, M04 Secondary Workflow Slice head) as ten descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H13), Claude review packet (C13), and CP00-394 handoff, without opening payments, payment matching, AR aging, or journal entry runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-393 is a descriptor-only RP13 Payments foundation pack (150 units, RP13.P01.M06.S01-RP13.P02.M04.S06, risk C) that closes the RP13.P01 model-foundation phase and opens the RP13.P02 foundation. Read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claim is supported and correctly gated. Coverage is consistent across every layer: the live plan (closeout-pack-plan.json:411), review-baseline.json, manifest.json, the registry requirements, validators, tests, contract, and README all agree on 150 units and the same distribution. I independently counted all nine deliverable types from the baseline plan (ui 23, implementation 77, test 17, security_audit 10, failure_recovery 6, contract 5, fixture 3, hermes_evidence 3, claude_review 6 = 150), matching the declared deliverable_distribution exactly. The changed source (registry.js, service.js, validators.js, index.js, model.test.js, validate-rp13-payments-core-contract.mjs, contract, README) contains only pure descriptor builders and validators with no filesystem/network/DB writes, no runtime execution, and no real-data loading; freezeCp393Result stamps every result with frozen no-write/no-runtime/no-leak/no-real-data constants. Authority boundaries are preserved: Claude is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob enforced in review-request.json) and never final approver (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false), and human final approval remains required. Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic evidence-packet/receipt descriptors (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime. Review hardening is satisfied: readiness auth preflight passed with non-zero stdout and valid JSON, only allowed tools, schema present, prompt within size; no forbidden review-evidence conditions (auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields). The pack correctly reports production_ready:false / status implemented_pending_review / runtime_ready:false, withholding the enterprise-trust claim until human approval. Validation outcomes are taken from recorded command-evidence.json (all commands exit 0), consistent with the read-only mandate, and the source code substantiates those results. No P0 or P1 issues. One non-blocking P3 hardening observation is noted for the downstream runtime-opening pack.

findings.title: Per-row descriptor guards do not extend to RP13.P02 service-cycle rows (by-design, non-blocking)
findings.details: validatePaymentsCoreCp393P01CloseoutP02FoundationDescriptor applies the four base assertions (descriptor_only, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false) to all 150 rows, but the targeted per-row guard loop (validators.js:326-352) only matches model-cycle row keys (tenant_scope_field, state_transition_map, validation_helper, fixture_model, model_unit_test, invalid_reference_test, hermes_model_summary, claude_model_review_prompt). RP13.P02 service-cycle rows such as permission_precheck, audit_hint_precheck, rollback_behavior and retry_behavior receive no dedicated per-row assertions and carry no extra guard fields (PAYMENTS_CORE_CP393_ROW_EXTRAS has no entries for these keys, so the spread resolves to {}). The no-write/no-leak/no-runtime boundary for these rows is instead guaranteed at the result level by freezeCp393Result (service.js:186-228), which sets evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_permission_decision, writes_audit_event, exposes_permission_decision_detail, exposes_audit_event_body, etc. to false as frozen constants that no input can flip. The boundary therefore holds by construction; this is consistent with the established CP00-392 pattern and is not a defect for a descriptor-only foundation pack.
findings.recommendation: No action required for CP00-393 closeout. When a downstream pack (CP00-394+) actually opens RP13.P02 permission/audit/failure-recovery runtime, extend the descriptor validator with explicit per-row assertions for permission_precheck, audit_hint_precheck, rollback_behavior, and retry_behavior so the guards are enforced by validation rather than relying solely on construction-level frozen constants.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking; acknowledged as by-design for descriptor-only scope and enforced by frozen constants. Recorded as a forward-looking hardening note for the downstream runtime-opening pack; does not block CP00-393 pack or goal closeout.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-393 payments core p01 closeout p02 foundation descriptor pack
